# EPIC 2026 — TRI-NET Scientific Improvement Plan (φ-anchor track)

> **Status:** `target` (plan), not a funded programme. All numbers and
> dates below are `target` / `projection` / `VERIFY` per the project's
> R5-honesty convention. See [`docs/SCIENTIFIC_IMPROVEMENT_PLAN.md`](../../docs/SCIENTIFIC_IMPROVEMENT_PLAN.md)
> for the authoritative spec — this directory contains the **filing
> material** that would create one GitHub epic + 16 child issues if /
> when a maintainer runs `create_issues.sh`.

## Identifier scheme (placeholders, not GitHub issue numbers)

This file uses **local plan IDs** of the form `#0` (epic), `#1` ..
`#16` (children). They are NOT GitHub issue numbers. GitHub will mint
real numbers when `create_issues.sh` is run; the README inside this
directory and the SIP itself refer to children by their **track ID**
(`CL-01`, `EN-02`, etc.) which is stable regardless of GitHub
numbering.

| Local ID | Track ID  | Title (short)                                            | Linked SIP section |
|---------:|-----------|----------------------------------------------------------|--------------------|
| `#0`     | EPIC      | TRI-NET 2026 Scientific Improvement Plan (φ-anchor)      | whole doc          |
| `#1`     | CL-01     | D2D drain-on-restraint normative                          | §2                 |
| `#2`     | CL-02     | Coq trace for Lucas-POST identity                         | §2                 |
| `#3`     | CL-03     | Ed25519 stub spec + host verifier reference               | §2                 |
| `#4`     | CL-04     | Per-die anchor-mismatch flag in conformance JSON          | §2                 |
| `#5`     | EN-01     | `specs/fpga/rbb_idle_path.t27` + `cap_boost.t27` stubs    | §3                 |
| `#6`     | EN-02     | `bench/22fdx/` projection notebook (reproducible band)    | §3                 |
| `#7`     | EN-03     | R5-honesty gate: refuse "1000×" / "4000 TOPS/W" restatement | §3               |
| `#8`     | SN-01     | Ternary round-trip row in NMSE harness                    | §4                 |
| `#9`     | SN-02     | Spike→D2D path documented as planned hook                 | §4                 |
| `#10`    | SN-03     | `tri-test.yml` SNN-burst anchor-stability job             | §4                 |
| `#11`    | PUB-01    | Whitepaper PDF upload to existing Zenodo DOI              | §5                 |
| `#12`    | PUB-02    | First `bench/nmse/<sha>.json` report                      | §5                 |
| `#13`    | PUB-03    | CLARA proof-trace note (CL-02 dependency)                 | §5                 |
| `#14`    | OS-01     | Preserve Apache-2.0 + open PDK posture (no relicense)     | §6                 |
| `#15`    | OS-02     | Reference host bridge in `examples/`                      | §6                 |
| `#16`    | OS-03     | `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md` with R5 checklist      | §6                 |

## Scope (φ-anchor specific)

This epic only covers what is owned by the **φ-anchor** track. It does
**not** create issues for the e-engine or γ-surface tracks — those
repos own their own filings.

## Out of scope (anti-claims)

- No funding state, programme dates, or contract status.
- No paper-acceptance claim — PUB-01..03 are draft-and-submit only.
- No silicon-return date — timeline marks silicon as `open`.
- No new DOI minted; only existing `10.5281/zenodo.19227877`.
- No measured TOPS/W; the `28–120 TOPS/W` band remains `projection`.

## How to file

A maintainer with `gh` authenticated against this repo can run
[`create_issues.sh`](create_issues.sh) (default: dry-run) and inspect
the `gh issue create` commands; re-run with `--apply` to actually
create the issues.

See [`ISSUES_SUMMARY.md`](ISSUES_SUMMARY.md) for the cross-table view.
